questions.question: What is the primary goal of random sampling?
options: A) To ensure representativeness
B) To identify patterns
C) To minimize bias
D) To maximize precision
questions.explanation: Random sampling is used to select a sample that is representative of the population.

questions.question: Which type of bias occurs when a sample is selected based on characteristics of the sample rather than the population?
options: A) Selection bias
B) Systematic bias
C) Random bias
D) Sampling bias
questions.explanation: Selection bias occurs when a sample is selected based on characteristics of the sample rather than the population.

questions.question: What is the central limit theorem?
options: A) A statistical technique for hypothesis testing
B) A property of the normal distribution
C) A concept in probability theory
D) A theorem describing the behavior of sampling distributions
questions.explanation: The central limit theorem states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normally distributed, regardless of the shape of the population distribution.

questions.question: What is the sampling distribution of a statistic?
options: A) A function of the sample data
B) A probability distribution of the sample statistic
C) A measure of the variability of the sample data
D) A summary statistic of the population data
questions.explanation: The sampling distribution of a statistic is a probability distribution that describes the distribution of the sample statistic, such as the mean or proportion, under repeated sampling from the population.

questions.question: Why is it important to consider the sampling distribution of a statistic?
options: A) To make inferences about the population mean
B) To make inferences about the population proportion
C) To understand the variability of the sample data
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Considering the sampling distribution of a statistic is important because it allows us to make inferences about the population parameters, such as the mean or proportion, with a certain degree of confidence.
